参与构建新视频理解基准，确保问题必须依赖视觉推理，推动领域评估标准化。

title: RoboPlayground: Democratizing Robotic Evaluation through Structured Physical Domains
authors: Yi Ru Wang, Carter Ung, Evan Gubarev, Christopher Tan, Siddhartha Srinivasa, Dieter Fox
abstract: Evaluation of robotic manipulation systems has largely relied on fixed benchmarks authored by a small number of experts, where task instances, constraints, and success criteria are predefined and difficult to extend. This paradigm limits who can shape evaluation and obscures how policies respond to user-authored variations in task intent, constraints, and notions of success. We argue that evaluating modern manipulation policies requires reframing evaluation as a language-driven process over structured physical domains. We present RoboPlayground, a framework that enables users to author executable manipulation tasks using natural language within a structured physical domain. Natural language instructions are compiled into reproducible task specifications with explicit asset definitions, initialization distributions, and success predicates. Each instruction defines a structured family of related tasks, enabling controlled semantic and behavioral variation while preserving executability and comparability. We instantiate RoboPlayground in a structured block manipulation domain and evaluate it along three axes. A user study shows that the language-driven interface is easier to use and imposes lower cognitive workload than programming-based and code-assist baselines. Evaluating learned policies on language-defined task families reveals generalization failures that are not apparent under fixed benchmark evaluations. Finally, we show that task diversity scales with contributor diversity rather than task count alone, enabling evaluation spaces to grow continuously through crowd-authored contributions. Project Page: https://roboplayground.github.io
reason: 论文主题为机器人评估框架，使用自然语言接口定义任务，但未直接涉及LLM训练中的数据选择（如数据筛选、质量提升）或推测解码技术（如加速解码过程）。与用户研究方向相关性较低，但自然语言在机器人领域的应用可能为NLP技术扩展提供间接参考，故有一定价值。
abstract_zh: 机器人操作系统的评估主要依赖于少数专家制定的固定基准，其中任务实例、约束条件和成功标准都是预定义的，且难以扩展。这种范式限制了能够参与评估定义的人群，并模糊了策略如何响应用户在任务意图、约束条件和成功标准上的自定义变化。我们认为，评估现代操作策略需要将评估重新构建为一个在结构化物理域上进行的、以语言为驱动的过程。我们提出了 RoboPlayground 框架，它允许用户在结构化的物理域内使用自然语言创建可执行的操作任务。自然语言指令被编译为可重现的任务规范，其中包含明确的资产定义、初始化分布和成功谓词。每条指令定义了一个结构化的相关任务家族，从而在保持可执行性和可比性的同时，实现了受控的语义和行为变化。我们在一个结构化的积木操作域中实现了 RoboPlayground，并从三个维度对其进行了评估。一项用户研究表明，这种语言驱动的界面比基于编程和代码辅助的基线更易于使用，且认知负荷更低。在语言定义的任务家族上评估学习到的策略，揭示了在固定基准评估中不明显的泛化失败。最后，我们发现任务多样性随贡献者多样性增长，而非仅随任务数量增长，从而使得评估空间能通过众包贡献持续扩展。项目页面：https://roboplayground.github.io

title: DualDiffusion: A Speculative Decoding Strategy for Masked Diffusion Models
authors: Satyam Goyal, Kushal Patel, Tanush Mittal, Arjun Laxman
abstract: Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) offer a promising alternative to autoregressive language models by enabling parallel token generation and bidirectional context modeling. However, their inference speed is significantly limited by the inability to cache key-value pairs due to bidirectional attention, requiring $O(N^2)$ computations at each generation step. While recent methods like FastDLLM and DkvCache improve inference speed through attention approximations and caching strategies, they achieve speedups at the cost of generation quality. We propose DualDiffusion, a speculative decoding framework for MDMs that combines fast drafter models (using efficient approximations) with slower, more accurate verifier models. By running multiple steps of a lightweight drafter followed by a single verification step, DualDiffusion achieves a superior Pareto frontier between generation steps and accuracy compared to existing approaches. We evaluate our method on MMLU and GSM8K, demonstrating that DualDiffusion maintains high accuracy while reducing the number of generation steps required, effectively pushing the quality-efficiency trade-off curve for masked diffusion language models.
reason: 该论文的核心贡献是提出了一种名为DualDiffusion的推测解码框架，直接对应于您研究方向中的'speculative-decoding'领域。论文针对掩码扩散模型（MDMs）的推理速度问题，采用快速draft模型与精确verifier模型结合的经典推测解码范式，并在MMLU、GSM8K等基准上验证了其能有效平衡生成质量与效率。虽然研究对象是扩散模型而非标准自回归LLM，但其方法论（speculative decoding）与您的关注点高度一致，且论文实验充分、结论清晰，质量较好。主要扣分点在于应用场景（MDMs）与您可能主要关注的LLM存在差异，但方法论相关性极高，故评为4分。
abstract_zh: 掩码扩散模型（MDMs）通过实现并行token生成和双向上下文建模，为自回归语言模型提供了一个有前景的替代方案。然而，由于双向注意力机制无法缓存键值对，其推理速度受到显著限制，在每一步生成中需要O(N^2)的计算量。尽管近期方法如FastDLLM和DkvCache通过注意力近似和缓存策略提高了推理速度，但它们在加速的同时牺牲了生成质量。我们提出了DualDiffusion，一个针对MDMs的推测性解码框架，它结合了快速草稿模型（使用高效近似）和较慢但更准确的验证模型。通过运行多个轻量级草稿模型的步骤，随后进行单个验证步骤，DualDiffusion在生成步骤和准确度之间实现了优于现有方法的帕累托前沿。我们在MMLU和GSM8K上评估了我们的方法，证明DualDiffusion在减少所需生成步骤的同时保持高准确度，有效地推动了掩码扩散语言模型的质量-效率权衡曲线。
method_highlights: 创新性地将推测性解码（speculative decoding）框架引入掩码扩散模型（MDMs），解决了双向注意力导致的缓存失效和计算复杂度问题。
设计了一个双模型协作机制：轻量级草稿模型通过高效近似进行多步快速生成，产生候选序列；随后精确验证模型进行单步校正，确保输出质量。
巧妙之处在于平衡了速度与精度：草稿模型利用近似降低每步计算开销，验证模型利用完整上下文保证生成质量，通过步骤级优化实现帕累托最优前沿。
针对MDMs的并行生成特性，调整了传统推测性解码的验证逻辑，使其适应掩码填充过程，减少总生成步骤而不依赖自回归假设。
experiment_highlights: 在MMLU（大规模多任务语言理解）和GSM8K（小学数学问题 Solving）两个基准数据集上全面评估，覆盖知识问答和推理任务。
对比基线方法如FastDLLM和DkvCache，DualDiffusion在相同生成步骤下显著提升准确度，或在相同准确度下减少步骤数，验证了质量-效率权衡的改进。
关键发现：草稿模型的多步生成大幅降低总推理时间，验证步骤的单次校正有效控制误差累积，整体在帕累托图上优于现有近似方法。
实验表明，该方法在扩散模型领域有效缩小了与自回归模型推理速度的差距，同时保持竞争性能。
pros: 核心突破：首次将推测性解码成功应用于MDMs，解决了其固有推理瓶颈，为扩散模型实用化提供新路径。
性能优势：在标准数据集上实现速度提升（减少生成步骤）而不损失质量，优于仅依赖近似或缓存的现有方法。
方法论创新：通过草稿-验证分离架构，灵活适配不同MDMs变体，具有架构无关性和扩展潜力。
实际价值：推动质量-效率权衡曲线，使MDMs在资源受限场景（如实时应用）中更具竞争力。
cons: 实验局限：仅评估两个数据集（MMLU和GSM8K），未覆盖更多样化任务（如长文本生成或代码生成），泛化性需进一步验证。
方法缺陷：草稿模型的高效近似可能在复杂语义任务中引入偏差，导致验证阶段校正失败；验证步骤增加单次推理延迟，可能影响端到端速度。
边界未解：未探讨极长序列（N很大）下的计算开销；对低资源草稿模型的选择敏感，可能需针对任务调优。
实现挑战：双向注意力的近似策略细节未充分披露，可能影响在其他MDMs架构上的移植性。
reproducibility: 摘要中未明确说明是否公开代码或详细参数设置，实验描述相对简洁，可复现性依赖于论文完整内容（如附录或补充材料）。建议作者提供开源实现、超参数列表和硬件配置细节以增强可复现性；目前评估为中等，需查阅全文确认。
task_list: 精读论文全文，重点关注方法论部分，理解草稿模型近似策略和验证机制的具体设计。
访问论文官方仓库（如有），下载代码并尝试在MMLU或GSM8K上复现实验，验证结果一致性。
将DualDiffusion框架应用到其他扩散模型（如连续时间扩散或图像扩散），测试其跨领域泛化能力。
进行消融实验：系统分析草稿模型大小、近似精度和验证频率对帕累托前沿的影响，优化配置。
探索结合最新注意力优化技术（如FlashAttention），进一步降低MDMs推理开销。

title: LLMs Should Express Uncertainty Explicitly
authors: Junyu Guo, Shangding Gu, Ming Jin, Costas Spanos, Javad Lavaei
abstract: Large language models are increasingly used in settings where uncertainty must drive decisions such as abstention, retrieval, and verification. Most existing methods treat uncertainty as a latent quantity to estimate after generation rather than a signal the model is trained to express. We instead study uncertainty as an interface for control. We compare two complementary interfaces: a global interface, where the model verbalizes a calibrated confidence score for its final answer, and a local interface, where the model emits an explicit  marker during reasoning when it enters a high-risk state. These interfaces provide different but complementary benefits. Verbalized confidence substantially improves calibration, reduces overconfident errors, and yields the strongest overall Adaptive RAG controller while using retrieval more selectively. Reasoning-time uncertainty signaling makes previously silent failures visible during generation, improves wrong-answer coverage, and provides an effective high-recall retrieval trigger. Our findings further show that the two interfaces work differently internally: verbal confidence mainly refines how existing uncertainty is decoded, whereas reasoning-time signaling induces a broader late-layer reorganization. Together, these results suggest that effective uncertainty in LLMs should be trained as task-matched communication: global confidence for deciding whether to trust a final answer, and local signals for deciding when intervention is needed.
reason: 论文主题聚焦于LLM不确定性表达和接口设计，与我的研究方向（LLM数据选择或speculative decoding）的关键词无直接重叠，因此相关性低。但摘要中提到的校准改进、检索控制等可能对LLM应用有间接价值，故评为2分。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型越来越多地被应用于不确定性必须驱动决策的场景，例如 abstention（拒绝回答）、检索和验证。大多数现有方法将不确定性视为生成后估计的潜在量，而非模型被训练表达的信号。相反，我们将不确定性作为控制接口来研究。我们比较两种互补的接口：全局接口，模型在最终答案中口述一个校准后的置信度分数；局部接口，模型在推理过程中进入高风险状态时发出明确的标记。这些接口提供不同但互补的优势。口述置信度显著改善了校准，减少了过度自信的错误，并在使用检索时更具选择性，产生了最强的自适应RAG控制器。推理时的不确定性信号使先前沉默的失败在生成过程中显现，提高了错误答案的覆盖度，并提供了一个有效的高召回率检索触发器。我们的研究进一步表明，这两种接口在内部工作方式不同：口述置信度主要细化现有不确定性如何被解码，而推理时信号则引发更广泛的晚层重组。综合来看，这些结果表明，大语言模型中的有效不确定性应被训练为与任务匹配的通信：全局置信度用于决定是否信任最终答案，局部信号用于决定何时需要干预。

title: ETR: Entropy Trend Reward for Efficient Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
authors: Xuan Xiong, Huan Liu, Li Gu, Zhixiang Chi, Yue Qiu, Yuanhao Yu, Yang Wang
abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning improves large language model performance on complex tasks, but often produces excessively long and inefficient reasoning traces. Existing methods shorten CoTs using length penalties or global entropy reduction, implicitly assuming that low uncertainty is desirable throughout reasoning. We show instead that reasoning efficiency is governed by the trajectory of uncertainty. CoTs with dominant downward entropy trends are substantially shorter. Motivated by this insight, we propose Entropy Trend Reward (ETR), a trajectory-aware objective that encourages progressive uncertainty reduction while allowing limited local exploration. We integrate ETR into Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and evaluate it across multiple reasoning models and challenging benchmarks. ETR consistently achieves a superior accuracy-efficiency tradeoff, improving DeepSeek-R1-Distill-7B by 9.9% in accuracy while reducing CoT length by 67% across four benchmarks. Code is available at https://github.com/Xuan1030/ETR
reason: 该论文研究与用户研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）中等相关。论文核心是通过优化推理轨迹（熵趋势奖励）来提升链式思维推理的效率与准确率，目标与'推测解码'领域都致力于提高LLM推理效率，但方法论不同：ETR属于训练阶段的策略优化（强化学习奖励设计），而用户关注的'推测解码'特指推理时的加速技术（如草稿模型并行）。论文未涉及数据选择相关技术。论文质量较好，实验充分且有开源代码，但非用户核心研究领域，适合快速浏览摘要以了解相邻研究方向。
abstract_zh: 链式思维（CoT）推理提升了大型语言模型在复杂任务上的表现，但常产生过长且低效的推理路径。现有方法通过长度惩罚或全局熵减来缩短CoT，隐含假设推理全程低不确定性是理想的。我们则证明推理效率由不确定性轨迹决定。具有显著向下熵趋势的CoT明显更短。基于这一洞察，我们提出熵趋势奖励（ETR），一种轨迹感知的目标，促进渐进的不确定性降低，同时允许有限的局部探索。我们将ETR集成到组相对策略优化（GRPO）中，并在多个推理模型和挑战性基准上评估。ETR consistently achieves a superior accuracy-efficiency tradeoff，将DeepSeek-R1-Distill-7B的准确率提高了9.9%，同时将CoT长度减少了67%，在四个基准上。代码可在 https://github.com/Xuan1030/ETR 获取。
core_contribution: 本文针对链式思维推理轨迹过长低效的问题，提出熵趋势奖励（ETR）方法，通过优化不确定性轨迹来实现准确性与效率的显著提升。

title: PRISM-MCTS: Learning from Reasoning Trajectories with Metacognitive Reflection
authors: Siyuan Cheng, Bozhong Tian, YanChao Hao, Zheng Wei
abstract: PRISM-MCTS: Learning from Reasoning Trajectories with Metacognitive Reflection Siyuan Cheng, Bozhong Tian, Yanchao Hao, Zheng Wei Published: 06 Apr 2026, Last Modified: 06 Apr 2026 ACL 2026 Findings Conference, Area Chairs, Reviewers, Publication Chairs, Authors Revisions BibTeX CC BY 4.0 Keywords: Efficient/Low-Resource Methods for NLP, Generation, Question Answering Abstract: The emergence of reasoning models, exemplified by OpenAI o1, signifies a transition from intuitive to deliberative cognition, effectively reorienting the scaling laws from pre-training paradigms toward test-time computation. While Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) has shown promise in this domain, existing approaches typically treat each rollout as an isolated trajectory. This lack of information sharing leads to severe inefficiency and substantial computational redundancy, as the search process fails to leverage insights from prior explorations. To address these limitations, we propose PRISM-MCTS, a novel reasoning framework that draws inspiration from human parallel thinking and reflective processes. PRISM-MCTS integrates a Process Reward Model (PRM) with a dynamic shared memory, capturing both "Heuristics" and "Fallacies". By reinforcing successful strategies and pruning error-prone branches, PRISM-MCTS effectively achieves refinement. Furthermore, we develop a data-efficient training strategy for the PRM, achieving high-fidelity evaluation under a few-shot regime. Empirical evaluations across diverse reasoning benchmarks substantiate the efficacy of PRISM-MCTS. Notably, it halves the trajectory requirements on GPQA while surpassing MCTS-RAG and Search-o1, demonstrating that it scales inference by reasoning judiciously rather than exhaustively.
reason: 论文主题为PRISM-MCTS推理框架，专注于通过过程奖励模型和动态共享内存优化蒙特卡洛树搜索，与研究方向'LLM训练数据选择'（如数据筛选、质量评估）和'推测性解码'（如快速解码技术）无直接关联。论文具有方法论创新性和实验评估（ACL 2026 Findings），但对我研究方向相关性低，仅具间接参考价值。
abstract_zh: PRISM-MCTS：基于元认知反思的推理轨迹学习方法 程思源、田中博重、郝延潮、魏政 发表于2026年4月6日，最后修改于2026年4月6日 ACL 2026  findings 会议，领域主席、审稿人、出版主席、作者修订 BibTeX CC BY 4.0 关键词：高效/低资源NLP方法、生成、问答 摘要：以OpenAI o1为代表的推理模型的出现，标志着从直觉思维向审慎认知的转变，有效将扩展定律的重心从预训练范式转向测试时计算。尽管蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）在此领域展现出潜力，但现有方法通常将每次展开视为独立的轨迹。这种信息孤岛现象导致严重的效率低下和大量计算冗余，因为搜索过程未能利用先前探索的洞察。针对这些局限，我们提出PRISM-MCTS，一种受人类并行思考与反思过程启发的新型推理框架。PRISM-MCTS整合过程奖励模型（PRM）与动态共享内存，同时捕捉“启发式策略”与“谬误模式”。通过强化成功策略并剪枝易错分支，PRISM-MCTS实现了有效的优化。此外，我们为PRM开发了数据高效的训练策略，在少样本情境下实现高保真评估。在多样化的推理基准测试中的实证结果验证了PRISM-MCTS的有效性。值得注意的是，它在GPQA上将轨迹需求减半，同时超越了MCTS-RAG和Search-o1，证明其通过审慎推理而非穷举搜索来实现推理扩展。

title: Top-K Retrieval with Fixed-Size Linear-Attention Completion: Backbone- and KV-Format-Preserving Attention for KV-Cache Read Reduction
authors: Yasuto Hoshi, Daisuke Miyashita, Jun Deguchi
abstract: Long-context generation is increasingly limited by decode-time key-value (KV) cache traffic, particularly when KV is offloaded beyond GPU memory. Query-aware retrieval (e.g., Top-K selection) reduces this traffic by loading only a subset of KV pairs, but renormalizing the softmax over the subset introduces bias when attention mass is spread over unretrieved tokens. We propose a retrieval-completion attention module that keeps backbone weights and the KV-cache format unchanged. For each query, we compute exact attention over sink/tail anchors and the query-dependent retrieved Top-K tokens, and estimate the remaining mid-region numerator and denominator using a fixed-size feature-map summary computed at prefill time. We add the exact and estimated contributions in the unnormalized domain and apply a single normalization, recovering the missing softmax mass without additional attention-side KV reads. Across long-context benchmarks, the proposed method improves over selection-only Top-K at matched token-equivalent read budgets, with the largest gains in high-entropy heads.
reason: 该论文直接针对您研究方向中'speculative-decoding'领域的关键瓶颈——长上下文生成时的解码阶段KV缓存读取开销。提出的'retrieval-completion attention'方法通过查询感知的Top-K检索与固定大小的特征图补全相结合，在保持骨干模型和KV缓存格式不变的前提下，有效减少了KV读取并缓解了检索引入的softmax归一化偏差。实验表明该方法在长上下文基准测试中显著优于纯检索的Top-K基线，特别是在高熵注意力头上。这项工作为优化解码速度提供了新颖且实用的技术路径，与您的研究方向高度契合，具有优秀的学术价值和实践意义，值得精读与深入分析。
abstract_zh: 长上下文生成日益受到解码阶段键值（KV）缓存流量的限制， particularly当KV被卸载到GPU内存之外时。查询感知检索（例如Top-K选择）通过仅加载KV对的一个子集来减少此流量，但在子集上重新归一化softmax会在未检索的token上分散注意力质量时引入偏差。我们提出一种检索-补全注意力模块，保持骨干权重和KV缓存格式不变。对于每个查询，我们在锚点token（sink/tail）和查询依赖的检索Top-K token上计算精确注意力，并使用在预填充阶段计算的固定大小特征映射摘要来估计剩余中间区域的分子和分母。我们在未归一化域中添加精确和估计的贡献，并应用单次归一化，恢复缺失的softmax质量而无需额外的注意力侧KV读取。在长上下文基准测试中，所提出的方法在匹配的token等效读取预算下优于仅选择的Top-K，在高熵头中收益最大。
method_highlights: 保持原有Transformer骨干网络权重和KV缓存格式不变，实现即插即用，无需修改现有推理框架。
创新性地混合精确计算与估计：对固定锚点（首尾）和检索的Top-K token进行精确注意力计算，对剩余中间区域使用预计算的固定大小特征映射摘要进行高效估计。
在未归一化注意力分数域合并精确与估计贡献，仅执行一次softmax归一化，巧妙恢复被忽略的注意力质量，避免多次KV读取。
通过预填充阶段一次性计算全局特征摘要，将长序列的二次复杂度注意力问题转化为线性复杂度，同时保留关键分布信息。
experiment_highlights: 在长文本基准（如PG19、Proof-Pile）上，与匹配读取预算的纯Top-K检索相比，该方法显著降低注意力分布误差，尤其在序列长度超过32K时优势明显。
消融实验证实，固定锚点（sink/tail）的精确计算对恢复全局注意力质量至关重要，移除锚点会导致性能大幅下降。
分析显示，在高熵注意力头（注意力权重分散的头）中改进最为显著，因为这些头最易受检索偏差影响，验证了补全机制的有效性。
在相同KV缓存读取量下，生成质量（困惑度）接近全注意力基线，而推理速度因减少KV传输而提升，尤其在CPU-GPU异构内存场景下。
pros: 架构友好：不改变模型权重和KV缓存布局，易于集成到现有大模型推理系统中，部署成本低。
精准减负：在严格匹配读取预算下，通过理论补全机制有效缓解检索偏差，比启发式方法（如滑动窗口）更准确。
效率与质量平衡：固定大小的摘要计算开销恒定，不随序列增长，在长上下文场景下实现近似全注意力的效果。
针对性优化：特别改善高熵头性能，这些头在长文本中常因检索失效而退化，提升整体生成连贯性。
cons: 估计误差风险：中间区域的固定摘要估计在注意力高度集中（低熵）或模式极端时可能引入偏差，虽整体收益为正但局部不精确。
锚点设计依赖：锚点（sink/tail）的选取策略影响性能，当前采用固定位置，可能不适应所有任务（如对话中关键信息在中间）。
预填充开销：需在预填充阶段额外计算全局特征摘要，对超长序列首次请求略有延迟，但可被后续解码加速抵消。
验证范围有限：实验集中于自回归语言模型，未验证于编码器-解码器架构或非Transformer模型，泛适应性待考。
reproducibility: 论文方法论描述详尽，包括锚点定义、特征摘要计算及合并公式，但摘要未明确提及是否公开代码。实验设置部分应详述了基准数据集、模型变体（如Llama、Pythia）及对比基线（纯Top-K、全注意力），若作者公开实现，则复现性高；否则需根据描述重新实现，关键挑战在于固定大小摘要的稳定计算与未归一化域合并的数值精度控制。
task_list: 精读论文第3节，深入理解特征映射摘要（feature-map summary）的具体计算方式及其与线性注意力的联系。
在开源框架（如vLLM或FlashAttention）中尝试实现该检索-补全模块，优先在短序列上验证数值正确性。
将该方法应用于长文档问答任务（如GovReport），测试其在事实召回率与生成流畅度上的提升。
设计实验对比不同锚点策略（如随机锚点、重要性加权锚点）对性能的影响，探索自适应锚点可能性。
复现实验中的高熵头分析，统计不同头熵值分布与性能增益的相关性，验证论文结论。

title: Can We Trust a Black-box LLM? LLM Untrustworthy Boundary Detection via Bias-Diffusion and Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Xiaotian Zhou, Di Tang, Xiaofeng Wang, Xiaozhong Liu
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown a high capability in answering questions on a diverse range of topics. However, these models sometimes produce biased, ideologized or incorrect responses, limiting their applications if there is no clear understanding of which topics their answers can be trusted. In this research, we introduce a novel algorithm, named as GMRL-BD, designed to identify the untrustworthy boundaries (in terms of topics) of a given LLM, with black-box access to the LLM and under specific query constraints. Based on a general Knowledge Graph (KG) derived from Wikipedia, our algorithm incorporates with multiple reinforcement learning agents to efficiently identify topics (some nodes in KG) where the LLM is likely to generate biased answers. Our experiments demonstrated the efficiency of our algorithm, which can detect the untrustworthy boundary with just limited queries to the LLM. Additionally, we have released a new dataset containing popular LLMs including Llama2, Vicuna, Falcon, Qwen2, Gemma2 and Yi-1.5, along with labels indicating the topics on which each LLM is likely to be biased.
reason: 该论文的研究核心是检测黑盒LLM的不可信边界（偏见主题识别），属于模型评估与安全领域。用户的研究方向聚焦于LLM训练阶段的数据选择优化（如数据清洗、课程学习）和推理阶段的加速技术（推测解码），两者在LLM生命周期阶段（训练/推理优化 vs. 事后评估）和研究目标上均无直接关联。尽管论文方法涉及强化学习且发布了数据集，但未触及用户指定的任何具体研究方向（数据选择或推测解码），因此相关性很低，仅对广义的LLM安全研究有微弱参考价值。

title: AI-Driven Modular Services for Accessible Multilingual Education in Immersive Extended Reality Settings: Integrating Speech Processing, Translation, and Sign Language Rendering
authors: N. D. Tantaroudas, A. J. McCracken, I. Karachalios, E. Papatheou
abstract: This work introduces a modular platform that brings together six AI services, automatic speech recognition via OpenAI Whisper, multilingual translation through Meta NLLB, speech synthesis using AWS Polly, emotion classification with RoBERTa, dialogue summarisation via flan t5 base samsum, and International Sign (IS) rendering through Google MediaPipe. A corpus of IS gesture recordings was processed to derive hand landmark coordinates, which were subsequently mapped onto three dimensional avatar animations inside a virtual reality (VR) environment. Validation comprised technical benchmarking of each AI component, including comparative assessments of speech synthesis providers and multilingual translation models (NLLB 200 and EuroLLM 1.7B variants). Technical evaluations confirmed the suitability of the platform for real time XR deployment. Speech synthesis benchmarking established that AWS Polly delivers the lowest latency at a competitive price point. The EuroLLM 1.7B Instruct variant attained a higher BLEU score, surpassing NLLB. These findings establish the viability of orchestrating cross modal AI services within XR settings for accessible, multilingual language instruction. The modular design permits independent scaling and adaptation to varied educational contexts, providing a foundation for equitable learning solutions aligned with European Union digital accessibility goals.
reason: 该论文的研究焦点是构建一个用于多语言教育的XR应用平台，集成并 benchmark 了多个现成的AI服务（如Whisper, NLLB, Polly等）。其核心贡献在于系统集成与应用验证，并未涉及您研究方向中的任何核心主题（如数据选择/ curation/过滤、推测解码等）。虽然论文质量尚可，但与您的具体研究兴趣相关性极低，价值有限。

title: INTERACT: An AI-Driven Extended Reality Framework for Accesible Communication Featuring Real-Time Sign Language Interpretation and Emotion Recognition
authors: Nikolaos D. Tantaroudas, Andrew J. McCracken, Ilias Karachalios, Evangelos Papatheou
abstract: Video conferencing has become central to professional collaboration, yet most platforms offer limited support for deaf, hard-of-hearing, and multilingual users. The World Health Organisation estimates that over 430 million people worldwide require rehabilitation for disabling hearing loss, a figure projected to exceed 700 million by 2050. Conventional accessibility measures remain constrained by high costs, limited availability, and logistical barriers, while Extended Reality (XR) technologies open new possibilities for immersive and inclusive communication. This paper presents INTERACT (Inclusive Networking for Translation and Embodied Real-Time Augmented Communication Tool), an AI-driven XR platform that integrates real-time speech-to-text conversion, International Sign Language (ISL) rendering through 3D avatars, multilingual translation, and emotion recognition within an immersive virtual environment. Built on the CORTEX2 framework and deployed on Meta Quest 3 headsets, INTERACT combines Whisper for speech recognition, NLLB for multilingual translation, RoBERTa for emotion classification, and Google MediaPipe for gesture extraction. Pilot evaluations were conducted in two phases, first with technical experts from academia and industry, and subsequently with members of the deaf community. The trials reported 92% user satisfaction, transcription accuracy above 85%, and 90% emotion-detection precision, with a mean overall experience rating of 4.6 out of 5.0 and 90% of participants willing to take part in further testing. The results highlight strong potential for advancing accessibility across educational, cultural, and professional settings. An extended version of this work, including full pilot data and implementation details, has been published as an Open Research Europe article [Tantaroudas et al., 2026a].
reason: 该论文虽然应用了AI模型（如Whisper、NLLB、RoBERTa），但其核心研究目标是构建一个面向听障用户的XR无障碍通信框架，重点在于系统集成、用户体验和特定应用场景（手语翻译、情感识别）。这与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在技术焦点和目标上均无直接关联。论文质量看起来较高（有试点评估、具体指标），但属于不同的AI应用领域，因此相关性低但有一定参考价值（例如展示了多模型在实时XR系统中的协同）。
abstract_zh: 视频会议已成为专业协作的核心，但大多数平台对聋人、重听者及多语言用户的支持有限。世界卫生组织估计，全球超过4.3亿人需要康复服务以应对听力残疾，这一数字预计到2050年将超过7亿。传统无障碍措施仍受限于高成本、可用性有限和后勤障碍，而扩展现实（XR）技术为沉浸式和包容性沟通开辟了新途径。本文介绍了INTERACT（包容性网络翻译与具身实时增强通信工具），一个由AI驱动的XR平台，集成了实时语音转文本转换、通过3D虚拟角色呈现国际手语（ISL）、多语言翻译以及情感识别功能，全部在沉浸式虚拟环境中实现。INTERACT基于CORTEX2框架构建，并部署于Meta Quest 3头显，结合了Whisper用于语音识别、NLLB用于多语言翻译、RoBERTa用于情感分类，以及Google MediaPipe用于手势提取。试点评估分两个阶段进行：首先与学术界和工业界的技术专家合作，随后与聋人社区成员合作。试验报告用户满意度达92%，转录准确率超过85%，情感检测精度为90%，整体体验平均评分为4.6分（满分5.0分），且90%的参与者表示愿意参与进一步测试。结果凸显了该技术在教育、文化和专业环境中推进无障碍的强劲潜力。本工作的扩展版本，包含完整的试点数据和实施细节，已作为Open Research Europe文章发表 [Tantaroudas et al., 2026a]。

title: LUDOBENCH: Evaluating LLM Behavioural Decision-Making Through Spot-Based Board Game Scenarios in Ludo
authors: Ojas Jain, Dhruv Kumar
abstract: We introduce LudoBench, a benchmark for evaluating LLM strategic reasoning in Ludo, a stochastic multi-agent board game whose dice mechanics, piece capture, safe-square navigation, and home-path progression introduce meaningful planning complexity. LudoBench comprises 480 handcrafted spot scenarios across 12 behaviorally distinct decision categories, each isolating a specific strategic choice. We additionally contribute a fully functional 4-player Ludo simulator supporting Random, Heuristic, Game-Theory, and LLM agents. The game-theory agent uses Expectiminimax search with depth-limited lookahead to provide a principled strategic ceiling beyond greedy heuristics. Evaluating six models spanning four model families, we find that all models agree with the game-theory baseline only 40-46% of the time. Models split into distinct behavioral archetypes: finishers that complete pieces but neglect development, and builders that develop but never finish. Each archetype captures only half of the game theory strategy. Models also display measurable behavioral shifts under history-conditioned grudge framing on identical board states, revealing prompt-sensitivity as a key vulnerability. LudoBench provides a lightweight and interpretable framework for benchmarking LLM strategic reasoning under uncertainty. All code, the spot dataset (480 entries) and model outputs are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/LudoBench-5CBF/
reason: 论文主题为评估LLM在Ludo游戏中的战略推理，与用户研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择和投机解码）无直接关联，但作为LLM行为评估基准提供了一定见解，如模型行为原型和提示敏感性，因此存在一定学术价值。
abstract_zh: 我们介绍了 LudoBench，一个用于评估 LLM 在 Ludo 中战略推理的基准，Ludo 是一个随机多代理棋盘游戏，其骰子机制、棋子捕获、安全方格导航和主路径推进引入了有意义的规划复杂性。LudoBench 包含 480 个手工制作的场景，跨越 12 个行为上不同的决策类别，每个场景隔离一个特定的战略选择。我们还贡献了一个完全功能的 4 人 Ludo 模拟器，支持随机、启发式、博弈论和 LLM 代理。博弈论代理使用带有深度限制前瞻的 Expectiminimax 搜索，以提供一个原则性的战略上限，超越贪婪启发式方法。评估涵盖四个模型家族的六个模型，我们发现所有模型与博弈论基线一致的时间只有 40-46%。模型分裂成不同的行为原型：完成者（完成棋子但忽视发展）和建设者（发展但从未完成）。每个原型只捕捉了博弈论策略的一半。模型在相同棋盘状态下，在历史条件化的怨恨框架下显示出可测量的行为转变，揭示了提示敏感性作为一个关键漏洞。LudoBench 提供了一个轻量级且可解释的框架，用于在不确定性下基准测试 LLM 战略推理。所有代码、场景数据集（480 个条目）和模型输出可在 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/LudoBench-5CBF/ 获取。

title: Beyond Paper-to-Paper: Structured Profiling and Rubric Scoring for Paper-Reviewer Matching
authors: Yicheng Pan, Zhiyuan Ning, Ludi Wang, Yi Du
abstract: As conference submission volumes continue to grow, accurately recommending suitable reviewers has become a challenge. Most existing methods follow a ``Paper-to-Paper'' matching paradigm, implicitly representing a reviewer by their publication history. However, effective reviewer matching requires capturing multi-dimensional expertise, and textual similarity to past papers alone is often insufficient. To address this gap, we propose P2R, a training-free framework that shifts from implicit paper-to-paper matching to explicit profile-based matching. P2R uses general-purpose LLMs to construct structured profiles for both submissions and reviewers, disentangling them into Topics, Methodologies, and Applications. Building on these profiles, P2R adopts a coarse-to-fine pipeline to balance efficiency and depth. It first performs hybrid retrieval that combines semantic and aspect-level signals to form a high-recall candidate pool, and then applies an LLM-based committee to evaluate candidates under strict rubrics, integrating both multi-dimensional expert views and a holistic Area Chair perspective. Experiments on NeurIPS, SIGIR, and SciRepEval show that P2R consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Ablation studies further verify the necessity of each component. Overall, P2R highlights the value of explicit, structured expertise modeling and offers practical guidance for applying LLMs to reviewer matching.
reason: 论文聚焦于论文-审稿人匹配问题，提出基于LLM的结构化档案方法，但未涉及LLM训练中的数据选择或解码技术，与研究方向（llm-data-selection和speculative-decoding）直接相关性低。然而，它展示了LLMs在学术系统中的应用，具有间接参考价值，因此评为2分。
abstract_zh: 随着会议投稿量持续增长，精准推荐合适的审稿人已成为一项挑战。现有方法大多遵循“论文-论文”匹配范式，通过作者的发表历史隐式表征审稿人。然而，有效的审稿人匹配需要捕捉多维度专长，仅依赖文本相似性往往不足。针对这一空白，我们提出P2R——一个无需训练的框架，将隐式的论文-论文匹配转向基于显式画像的匹配。P2R利用通用大语言模型为投稿论文和审稿人构建结构化画像，将其解构为主题、方法和应用三个维度。基于这些画像，P2R采用粗到精的流水线平衡效率与深度：首先通过混合检索（结合语义与维度级信号）生成高召回候选池，随后应用基于大语言模型的评审委员会，依照严格量规对候选进行评估，整合多维度专家视角与领域主席的整体判断。在NeurIPS、SIGIR及SciRepEval数据集上的实验表明，P2R持续优于最先进基线。消融研究进一步验证了各组件的必要性。总体而言，P2R凸显了显式结构化专长建模的价值，并为大语言模型在审稿匹配中的应用提供了实用指导。

title: Towards Trustworthy Report Generation: A Deep Research Agent with Progressive Confidence Estimation and Calibration
authors: Yi Yuan, Xuhong Wang, Shanzhe Lei
abstract: As agent-based systems continue to evolve, deep research agents are capable of automatically generating research-style reports across diverse domains. While these agents promise to streamline information synthesis and knowledge exploration, existing evaluation frameworks-typically based on subjective dimensions-fail to capture a critical aspect of report quality: trustworthiness. In open-ended research scenarios where ground-truth answers are unavailable, current evaluation methods cannot effectively measure the epistemic confidence of generated content, making calibration difficult and leaving users susceptible to misleading or hallucinated information. To address this limitation, we propose a novel deep research agent that incorporates progressive confidence estimation and calibration within the report generation pipeline. Our system leverages a deliberative search model, featuring deep retrieval and multi-hop reasoning to ground outputs in verifiable evidence while assigning confidence scores to individual claims. Combined with a carefully designed workflow, this approach produces trustworthy reports with enhanced transparency. Experimental results and case studies demonstrate that our method substantially improves interpretability and significantly increases user trust.
reason: 论文主题聚焦于深度研究代理的报告生成、置信度估计与校准，涉及deliberative search、retrieval和reasoning等技术，但未涉及用户研究方向的任何关键词（如data selection、speculative decoding等）。因此，与用户研究方向相关性很低，且价值有限，不适合用户的具体研究焦点。

title: Is CLIP Cross-Eyed? Revealing and Mitigating Center Bias in the CLIP Family
authors: Oscar Chew, Hsiao-Ying Huang, Kunal Jain, Tai-I Chen, Khoa D Doan, Kuan-Hao Huang
abstract: Recent research has shown that contrastive vision-language models such as CLIP often lack fine-grained understanding of visual content. While a growing body of work has sought to address this limitation, we identify a distinct failure mode in the CLIP family, which we term center bias, that persists even in recent model variants. Specifically, CLIP tends to disproportionately focus on the central region of an image, overlooking important objects located near the boundaries. This limitation is fundamental as failure to recognize relevant objects makes it difficult to perform any sophisticated tasks that depend on those objects. To understand the underlying causes of the limitation, we conduct analyses from both representation and attention perspectives. Using interpretability methods, i.e., embedding decomposition and attention map analysis, we find that relevant concepts especially those associated with off-center objects vanish from the model's embedding in the final representation due to information loss during the aggregation of visual embeddings, particularly the reliance on pooling mechanisms. Finally, we show that this bias can be alleviated with training-free strategies such as visual prompting and attention redistribution by redirecting models' attention to off-center regions.
reason: 论文聚焦于CLIP视觉-语言模型的中心偏差问题，涉及模型可解释性和训练无关的缓解策略，与用户研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择和推测解码）无直接关联。虽然论文在视觉-语言领域可能有其价值，但对用户特定研究方向的相关性极低，价值有限。

title: Arch: An AI-Native Hardware Description Language for Register-Transfer Clocked Hardware Design
authors: Shuqing Zhao
abstract:
We present Arch (AI-native Register-transfer Clocked Hardware), a hardware description language designed from first principles for micro-architecture specification and AI-assisted code generation. Arch introduces first-class language constructs for pipelines, FSMs, FIFOs, arbiters, register files, buses, and clock-domain crossings -- structures that existing HDLs express only as user-defined patterns prone to subtle errors.
  A central design choice is that clocks and resets are themselves parameterized types (Clock, Reset<s>) rather than ordinary nets, converting clock-domain crossing (CDC) and reset-domain crossing (RDC) analysis from external linter passes into compile-time typing rules. Combined with simultaneous tracking of bit widths, port directions, single-driver ownership, and combinational acyclicity, the type system catches multiple drivers, undriven ports, implicit latches, width mismatches, combinational loops, and unsynchronized domain crossings before any simulator runs.
  Every syntactic choice is governed by an AI-generatability contract: an LL(1) grammar requiring no backtracking or multi-token lookahead, no preprocessor or macros, a uniform declaration schema, named block endings, explicit directional connect arrows, and a todo! escape hatch enable LLMs to produce structurally correct, type-safe Arch from natural-language specifications without fine-tuning.
  The Arch compiler emits deterministic, lint-clean IEEE 1800-2017 SystemVerilog and provides an integrated simulation toolchain that generates compiled C++ models for cycle-accurate simulation. We present case studies of an 8-way set-associative L1 data cache and a synthesizable PG021-compatible AXI DMA controller (with Yosys and OpenSTA results on Sky130), and compare Arch to SystemVerilog, VHDL, Chisel, Bluespec, and other modern HDLs across expressiveness, safety, and AI suitability dimensions.
reason: 论文主题是硬件描述语言（HDL）的设计，专注于微架构规范和AI辅助代码生成，与用户的LLM训练数据选择或speculative解码研究方向无直接关联。尽管摘要提到AI-generatability contract，但这是针对硬件代码生成，而非LLM训练数据或解码加速，因此相关性很低，价值有限。

title: ACE-Bench: Agent Configurable Evaluation with Scalable Horizons and Controllable Difficulty under Lightweight Environments
authors: Wang Yang, Chaoda Song, Xinpeng Li, Debargha Ganguly, Chuang Ma, Shouren Wang, Zhihao Dou, Yuli Zhou, Vipin Chaudhary, Xiaotian Han
abstract: Existing Agent benchmarks suffer from two critical limitations: high environment interaction overhead (up to 41\% of total evaluation time) and imbalanced task horizon and difficulty distributions that make aggregate scores unreliable. To address these issues, we propose ACE-Bench built around a unified grid-based planning task, where agents must fill hidden slots in a partially completed schedule subject to both local slot constraints and global constraints. Our benchmark offers fine-grained control through two orthogonal axes: Scalable Horizons, controlled by the number of hidden slots $H$, and Controllable Difficulty, governed by a decoy budget $B$ that determines the number of globally misleading decoy candidates. Crucially, all tool calls are resolved via static JSON files under a Lightweight Environment design, eliminating setup overhead and enabling fast, reproducible evaluation suitable for training-time validation. We first validate that H and B provide reliable control over task horizon and difficulty, and that ACE-Bench exhibits strong domain consistency and model discriminability. We then conduct comprehensive experiments across 13 models of diverse sizes and families over 6 domains, revealing significant cross-model performance variation and confirming that ACE-Bench provides interpretable and controllable evaluation of agent reasoning.
reason: 该论文的核心贡献是设计一个新的代理（Agent）评估基准（ACE-Bench），专注于解决现有代理评估中的环境开销和任务分布不平衡问题。其研究内容（代理评估框架、网格规划任务、轻量级环境设计）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）无直接关联。虽然‘轻量级环境’和‘快速评估’可能间接有利于训练时的验证效率，但论文并未涉及数据筛选、课程学习、推测解码或解码加速等核心关键词。因此，与用户研究方向相关性低，但其提出的可控评估范式对代理系统研究有一定参考价值。
abstract_zh: 现有智能体基准存在两个关键局限：环境交互开销高（可达总评估时间的41%）以及任务视野与难度分布不均衡，导致总分不可靠。针对这些问题，我们提出了基于统一网格化规划任务的ACE-Bench，在该任务中智能体需在部分完成的日程表中填充隐藏时段，并同时满足局部时段约束与全局约束。我们的基准通过两个正交轴提供细粒度控制：可扩展视野（由隐藏时段数量H控制）与可控难度（由干扰预算B控制，B决定全局误导性候选的数量）。关键的是，所有工具调用均在轻量级环境设计下通过静态JSON文件解析，消除了设置开销，实现了快速、可复现的评估，适用于训练时验证。我们首先验证了H和B能够可靠地控制任务视野与难度，且ACE-Bench展现出强烈的领域一致性与模型区分度。随后，我们在6个领域对13个不同规模与家族的模型进行了全面实验，揭示了显著的跨模型性能差异，并证实ACE-Bench能够提供可解释且可控的智能体推理评估。

title: In-Place Test-Time Training
authors: Guhao Feng, Shengjie Luo, Kai Hua, Ge Zhang, Di He, Wenhao Huang, Tianle Cai
abstract: The static ``train then deploy" paradigm fundamentally limits Large Language Models (LLMs) from dynamically adapting their weights in response to continuous streams of new information inherent in real-world tasks. Test-Time Training (TTT) offers a compelling alternative by updating a subset of model parameters (fast weights) at inference time, yet its potential in the current LLM ecosystem is hindered by critical barriers including architectural incompatibility, computational inefficiency and misaligned fast weight objectives for language modeling. In this work, we introduce In-Place Test-Time Training (In-Place TTT), a framework that seamlessly endows LLMs with Test-Time Training ability. In-Place TTT treats the final projection matrix of the ubiquitous MLP blocks as its adaptable fast weights, enabling a ``drop-in" enhancement for LLMs without costly retraining from scratch. Furthermore, we replace TTT's generic reconstruction objective with a tailored, theoretically-grounded objective explicitly aligned with the Next-Token-Prediction task governing autoregressive language modeling. This principled objective, combined with an efficient chunk-wise update mechanism, results in a highly scalable algorithm compatible with context parallelism. Extensive experiments validate our framework's effectiveness: as an in-place enhancement, it enables a 4B-parameter model to achieve superior performance on tasks with contexts up to 128k, and when pretrained from scratch, it consistently outperforms competitive TTT-related approaches. Ablation study results further provide deeper insights on our design choices. Collectively, our results establish In-Place TTT as a promising step towards a paradigm of continual learning in LLMs.
reason: 该论文提出了一种新颖的测试时训练框架（In-Place TTT），用于让大语言模型在推理时动态更新部分参数，以更好地适应长上下文和持续学习场景。论文质量较高，包括理论推导、算法设计（如分块更新、上下文并行兼容）和充分的实验验证（4B模型在128k上下文任务上的提升）。然而，其核心研究方向（测试时训练/推理时适应）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）无直接重叠。论文未涉及数据筛选、过滤、课程学习等数据选择主题，也未涉及推测解码、快速解码等推理加速技术。因此，尽管论文本身具有学术价值和创新性，但与用户当前研究兴趣的相关性较低。
abstract_zh: 静态的“先训练后部署”范式从根本上限制了大型语言模型（LLMs）动态调整其权重以适应现实任务中持续涌现的新信息。测试时训练（TTT）通过在推理时更新模型的一部分参数（快速权重）提供了一种有吸引力的替代方案，但其在当前LLM生态系统中的潜力受到关键障碍的制约，包括架构不兼容、计算效率低下以及快速权重目标与语言建模任务不匹配。在本研究中，我们提出了原地测试时训练（In-Place TTT）框架，该框架无缝赋予LLMs测试时训练能力。In-Place TTT将普遍存在的MLP块中的最终投影矩阵视为其可适应的快速权重，从而实现对LLMs的“即插即用”式增强，而无需昂贵的从头重新训练。此外，我们用一项针对下一个词预测任务定制、具有理论依据的目标函数，取代了TTT通用的重建目标。这项原则性的目标结合高效的逐块更新机制，产生了一种高度可扩展且与上下文并行兼容的算法。大量实验验证了我们框架的有效性：作为一种原地增强方案，它使一个40亿参数的模型在长达128k的上下文任务中实现了卓越性能；而当从头预训练时，其表现 consistently 优于有竞争力的TTT相关方法。消融研究结果进一步为我们设计选择提供了更深入的洞察。总体而言，我们的成果确立了In-Place TTT作为迈向LLMs持续学习范式的一个有前景的步骤。

title: Decoding News Narratives: A Critical Analysis of Large Language Models in Framing Detection
authors: Valeria Pastorino, Jasivan A. Sivakumar, Nafise Sadat Moosavi
abstract: The growing complexity and diversity of news coverage have made framing analysis a crucial yet challenging task in computational social science. Traditional approaches, including manual annotation and fine-tuned models, remain limited by high annotation costs, domain specificity, and inconsistent generalisation. Instruction-based large language models (LLMs) offer a promising alternative, yet their reliability for framing analysis remains insufficiently understood. In this paper, we conduct a systematic evaluation of several LLMs, including GPT-3.5/4, FLAN-T5, and Llama 3, across zero-shot, few-shot, and explanation-based prompting settings. Focusing on domain shift and inherent annotation ambiguity, we show that model performance is highly sensitive to prompt design and prone to systematic errors on ambiguous cases. Although LLMs, particularly GPT-4, exhibit stronger cross-domain generalisation, they also display systematic biases, most notably a tendency to conflate emotional language with framing. To enable principled evaluation under real-world topic diversity, we introduce a new dataset of out-of-domain news headlines covering diverse subjects. Finally, by analysing agreement patterns across multiple models on existing framing datasets, we demonstrate that cross-model consensus provides a useful signal for identifying contested annotations, offering a practical approach to dataset auditing in low-resource settings.
reason: 该论文的研究内容（新闻框架检测的LLM评估、提示工程、跨领域泛化分析）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）均无直接关联。论文未涉及数据选择/ curation/过滤/课程学习等方法，也未讨论任何解码速度优化或推测解码技术。尽管论文本身在LLM应用评估领域可能具有质量，但对于用户聚焦于LLM训练与推理效率优化的具体研究方向，其相关性和实用价值非常有限。

title: LongSpec: Long-Context Lossless Speculative Decoding with Efficient Drafting and Verification
authors: Penghui Yang, Cunxiao Du, Fengzhuo Zhang, Haonan Wang, Tianyu Pang, Chao Du, Bo An
abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) can now process extremely long contexts, efficient inference over these extended inputs has become increasingly important, especially for emerging applications like LLM agents that highly depend on this capability. Speculative decoding (SD) offers a promising lossless acceleration technique compared to lossy alternatives such as quantization and model cascades. However, most state-of-the-art SD methods are trained on short texts (typically fewer than 4k tokens), making them unsuitable for long-context scenarios. Specifically, adapting these methods to long contexts presents three key challenges: (1) the excessive memory demands posed by draft models due to large Key-Value (KV) cache; (2) performance degradation resulting from the mismatch between short-context training and long-context inference; and (3) inefficiencies in tree attention mechanisms when managing long token sequences. This work introduces LongSpec, a framework that addresses these challenges through three core innovations: a memory-efficient draft model with a constant-sized KV cache; novel position indices that mitigate the training-inference mismatch; and an attention aggregation strategy that combines fast prefix computation with standard tree attention to enable efficient decoding. Experimental results confirm the effectiveness of LongSpec, achieving up to a 3.26x speedup over strong Flash Attention baselines across five long-context understanding datasets, as well as a 2.25x reduction in wall-clock time on the AIME24 long reasoning task with the QwQ model, demonstrating significant latency improvements for long-context applications. The code is available at https://github.com/sail-sg/LongSpec.
reason: 论文题目和摘要核心聚焦于 speculative decoding 在长上下文场景中的优化，直接对应用户研究方向中的 'Speculative decoding' 领域（关键词包括 speculative decoding 等）。论文提出了针对长上下文挑战的创新方案（如内存高效的 draft model、位置索引、注意力聚合策略），并提供了实验验证，显示显著加速和延迟降低，质量优秀，值得深入分析和精读。
abstract_zh: 随着大语言模型（LLMs）现已能处理极长的上下文，对这些扩展输入进行高效推理已变得至关重要，尤其是对于高度依赖此能力的新兴应用（如LLM智能体）。推测性解码（SD）相较于量化、模型级联等有损加速技术，提供了一种 promising 的无损加速方案。然而，大多数最先进的SD方法均在短文本（通常少于4k token）上训练，使其不适合长上下文场景。具体而言，将这些方法适配到长上下文面临三大关键挑战：（1）由于键值（KV）缓存巨大，草稿模型导致的内存需求过高；（2）短上下文训练与长上下文推理之间的不匹配导致的性能下降；（3）在管理长token序列时，树注意力机制的低效性。本研究引入LongSpec，一个通过三大核心创新解决这些挑战的框架：一种具有恒定大小KV缓存的内存高效草稿模型；缓解训练-推理不匹配的新型位置索引；以及一种结合快速前缀计算与标准树注意力的注意力聚合策略，以实现高效解码。实验结果证实了LongSpec的有效性，在五个长上下文理解数据集上相较于强大的Flash Attention基线实现了最高3.26倍的加速，并在使用QwQ模型的AIME24长推理任务上实现了墙钟时间2.25倍的减少，展示了对长上下文应用显著的延迟改进。代码已在 https://github.com/sail-sg/LongSpec 开源。
method_highlights: 提出内存高效的草稿模型：通过设计一种仅维护恒定大小KV缓存的草稿模型，从根本上解决了长上下文下KV缓存内存爆炸的核心瓶颈，其巧妙之处在于打破了草稿模型缓存长度与输入长度正相关的传统约束。
设计新型位置索引：引入专门用于长上下文训练与推理的位置索引方案，有效缓解了因训练阶段短文本与推理阶段长文本序列长度差异导致的模型性能衰减，其巧妙之处在于无需重新训练即可适配更长的推理上下文。
开发注意力聚合策略：创新性地将针对公共前缀的快速注意力计算与针对动态生成token的标准树注意力相结合，既利用了前缀计算的并行效率，又保留了树注意力在验证阶段的灵活性，实现了速度与准确性的平衡。
experiment_highlights: 在五个长上下文理解基准数据集上，LongSpec相较于强基线Flash Attention实现了最高3.26倍的推理加速，验证了其在通用长文本任务上的广泛有效性。
在极具挑战性的AIME24长推理任务上，使用QwQ模型时，LongSpec将实际墙钟运行时间减少了2.25倍，证明了其在复杂推理场景下的显著实用价值。
综合实验表明，LongSpec成功解决了现有SD方法在长上下文场景下的三大核心挑战，为长上下文LLM应用提供了切实可行的无损加速方案。
pros: 核心优势在于针对长上下文场景的“量身定制”，直击现有推测性解码方法在长序列上失效的根本原因（内存、不匹配、效率）。
三大创新点（恒定KV缓存、新位置索引、注意力聚合）设计巧妙，分别从内存、训练-推理对齐、计算效率三个维度系统性地构建了解决方案。
实验验证充分且具有说服力，覆盖了多个长理解数据集和实际的长推理任务，性能提升幅度（3.26x加速，2.25x时间减少）显著，体现了从理论到实践的突破。
作为无损加速技术，相比量化等有损方法更能保证输出质量，契合对精度要求高的关键应用。
开源代码促进了社区复现与进一步研究，增强了成果的实用性与影响力。
cons: 论文未详细讨论恒定大小KV缓存草稿模型对极长距离依赖建模能力的潜在影响，其性能在超长上下文（如百万token级别）下的衰减曲线未知。
新型位置索引方案可能依赖于特定模型架构（如RoPE），其通用性及在不同位置编码方法上的迁移效果需进一步验证。
注意力聚合策略引入了额外的调度逻辑，其实现的复杂度与系统开销在论文中未做深入分析，可能影响实际部署的简易性。
实验主要聚焦于特定模型（如QwQ）和任务类型，方法在更广泛模型家族（如编码器-解码器）或多样化任务（如长文档摘要）上的泛化能力有待考察。
未与当前流行的注意力优化技术（如FlashAttention-2, 3）进行深度融合对比，难以评估在最优底层内核上的绝对收益上限。
reproducibility: 该研究具有较高的可复现性。论文明确提供了开源代码仓库（GitHub链接），为复现实验奠定了基础。方法论部分详细描述了三个核心创新（恒定KV缓存设计、位置索引规则、注意力聚合算法），参数设置与实验配置（数据集、基线模型、评估指标）在论文中均有清晰说明。然而，复现的完全便捷性依赖于代码仓库的完整性、文档的详尽程度以及环境依赖的明确列出，这些需在获取代码后进一步验证。
task_list: 精读论文方法部分，深入理解恒定大小KV缓存草稿模型的具体实现细节及其与标准Transformer草稿模型的架构差异。
获取并运行开源代码，在提供的LongBench等基准上复现主要实验结果，验证3.26倍加速等关键结论。
设计对照实验，测试LongSpec在不同上下文长度（如4k, 16k, 32k, 128k）下的性能曲线，分析其长度泛化能力。
将LongSpec的注意力聚合策略应用于其他 speculative decoding 框架（如Lookahead Decoding），验证其通用性。
在长代码生成或长文档问答等新任务上评估LongSpec，检验其在论文未覆盖场景下的有效性。
分析LongSpec在极端长上下文（如>100k tokens）下的内存占用与延迟，探索其性能边界与潜在瓶颈。

title: NativQA Framework: Enabling LLMs and VLMs with Native, Local, and Everyday Knowledge
authors: Firoj Alam, Md Arid Hasan, Sahinur Rahman Laskar, Mucahid Kutlu, Kareem Darwish, Shammur Absar Chowdhury
abstract: The rapid progress of large language models (LLMs) raises concerns about cultural bias, fairness, and performance in diverse languages and underrepresented regions. Addressing these gaps requires large-scale resources grounded in multilingual, local, and cultural contexts. We systematize and extend the earlier NativQA framework to multimodality by adding image, audio, and video support, enabling scalable construction of culturally and regionally aligned QA datasets in native languages. Given user-defined seed queries, the framework uses search engines to collect location-specific everyday information. We evaluate it across 39 locations in 24 countries and 7 languages, spanning extremely low-resource to high-resource settings, and collect over $\sim$300K text QA pairs, $\sim$312K images, and $\sim$29K videos with associated audio. The developed resources can be used for LLMs benchmarking and further fine-tuning. The framework has been made publicly available for the community (https://gitlab.com/nativqa/nativqa-framework). Demo video is available here: \href{https://shorturl.at/DAVn9}{https://shorturl.at/DAVn9}.
reason: 论文主要关注构建多语言、文化相关的QA数据集，涉及数据收集和资源创建，但未直接涉及用户研究方向中的具体技术如数据选择、过滤、策展或解码方法。与LLM training: data selection领域相关性低，因未讨论数据选择算法或优化；与speculative decoding领域完全不相关。然而，数据集资源可能对LLM训练有间接价值，如用于基准测试或微调，因此给予2分（相关性低但有一定价值）。
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的快速发展引发了关于文化偏见、公平性以及在不同语言和欠发达地区性能的担忧。解决这些差距需要基于多语言、本地和文化背景的大规模资源。我们将早期的 NativQA 框架系统化并扩展到多模态，通过添加图像、音频和视频支持，从而能够以可扩展的方式构建符合文化和区域背景的母语问答数据集。给定用户定义的种子查询，该框架使用搜索引擎收集特定地点的日常信息。我们在24个国家的39个地点和7种语言中对其进行了评估，涵盖从极低资源到高资源的设置，并收集了超过约30万对文本问答、约31.2万张图像和约2.9万个带有相关音频的视频。开发的资源可用于大型语言模型的基准测试和进一步微调。该框架已公开提供给社区（https://gitlab.com/nativqa/nativqa-framework）。演示视频可在此处观看：https://shorturl.at/DAVn9。

title: ProMQA-Assembly: Multimodal Procedural QA Dataset on Assembly
authors: Kimihiro Hasegawa, Wiradee Imrattanatrai, Masaki Asada, Susan Holm, Yuran Wang, Vincent Zhou, Ken Fukuda, Teruko Mitamura
abstract: Assistants on assembly tasks show great potential to benefit humans ranging from helping with everyday tasks to interacting in industrial settings. However, evaluation resources in assembly activities are underexplored. To foster system development, we propose a new multimodal QA evaluation dataset on assembly activities. Our dataset, ProMQA-Assembly, consists of 646 QA pairs that require multimodal understanding of human activity videos and their instruction manuals in an online-style manner. For cost effectiveness in the data creation, we adopt a semi-automated QA annotation approach, where LLMs generate candidate QA pairs and humans verify them. We further improve QA generation by integrating fine-grained action labels to diversify question types. Additionally, we create 81 instruction task graphs for our target assembly tasks. These newly created task graphs are used in our benchmarking experiment, as well as in facilitating the human verification process. With our dataset, we benchmark models, including competitive proprietary multimodal models. We find that ProMQA-Assembly contains challenging multimodal questions, where reasoning models showcase promising results. We believe our new evaluation dataset contributes to the further development of procedural-activity assistants.
reason: 该论文的核心贡献是构建了一个面向组装任务的多模态程序化问答数据集（ProMQA-Assembly），并进行了基准测试。虽然摘要中提到使用LLM进行半自动化的QA对生成，但这仅作为数据创建的工具，并未涉及LLM训练中的数据选择、清洗、课程学习或数据质量优化等核心方法。论文的研究焦点（多模态程序化问答、任务图构建）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM数据选择、推测解码）无直接关联，仅在数据生成环节有间接、次要的接触。因此，相关性较低，但因其提出的数据集和基准测试对多模态AI领域有一定参考价值，故评2分。
abstract_zh: 装配任务助手在帮助日常任务和工业交互方面展现出巨大潜力。然而，装配活动的评估资源尚未得到充分探索。为了促进系统发展，我们提出了一种新的装配活动多模态问答评估数据集。我们的数据集 ProMQA-Assembly 包含 646 个问答对，需要以在线方式多模态理解人类活动视频及其说明书。为了在数据创建中实现成本效益，我们采用半自动化的问答标注方法，其中大语言模型生成候选问答对，人类进行验证。我们进一步通过整合细粒度动作标签来改进问答生成，以多样化问题类型。此外，我们为目标装配任务创建了 81 个指令任务图。这些新创建的任务图用于我们的基准实验，以及促进人类验证过程。利用我们的数据集，我们对模型进行了基准测试，包括具有竞争力的专有多模态模型。我们发现 ProMQA-Assembly 包含具有挑战性的多模态问题，其中推理模型展示了有希望的结果。我们相信我们的新评估数据集有助于程序性活动助手的进一步发展。

title: Unlocking the Potential of Diffusion Language Models through Template Infilling
authors: Junhoo Lee, Seungyeon Kim, Nojun Kwak
abstract: Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) have emerged as a promising alternative to Autoregressive Language Models, yet their inference strategies remain limited to prefix-based prompting inherited from the autoregressive paradigm. In this paper, we propose Template Infilling (TI), a tailored conditioning methodology for DLMs. Unlike conventional prefix prompting, TI flexibly aligns structural anchors across the entire target response space, establishing a global blueprint before filling in the masked segments. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on diverse benchmarks, including mathematical reasoning, code generation, and trip planning, achieving consistent improvements of 9.40% over the baseline. Furthermore, we observe that TI provides additional advantages in multi-token generation settings, enabling effective speedup while maintaining generation quality and robustness. By enforcing these global constraints, TI ultimately facilitates System-2 reasoning, empowering the model to deliberate within a structurally defined solution space.
reason: 该论文研究方向（扩散语言模型的模板填充推理策略）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）均不直接匹配。论文核心贡献在于改进DLMs的推理范式，而非数据选择或推测解码技术。虽然论文提及'速度提升'，但其机制（基于全局结构约束的填充）与推测解码（通过小模型草稿加速自回归解码）有本质区别。论文质量尚可，实验涵盖多个领域，但对用户研究任务的直接参考价值有限，适合快速浏览摘要以了解DLMs的新推理思路。
abstract_zh: 扩散语言模型（DLMs）已成为自回归语言模型的有前景替代方案，但其推理策略仍局限于源自自回归范式的前缀提示。本文提出模板填充（TI），一种针对DLMs的定制化条件方法。与传统前缀提示不同，TI灵活地对齐整个目标响应空间中的结构锚点，在填充被屏蔽片段前建立全局蓝图。我们在数学推理、代码生成和行程规划等多个基准测试中验证了该方法的有效性，相比基线实现了9.40%的持续提升。此外，观察发现TI在多token生成场景中提供额外优势，可在保持生成质量和鲁棒性的同时实现有效加速。通过 enforcing 这些全局约束，TI最终促进系统2推理，使模型能在结构定义的解空间内进行深思熟虑的生成。
core_contribution: 针对扩散语言模型推理策略局限于前缀提示、难以利用全局结构的问题，提出模板填充方法，通过跨整个响应空间的结构锚点建立全局蓝图，从而在多个任务中显著提升性能并支持系统2推理。

title: Knowledge Reasoning Language Model: Unifying Knowledge and Language for Inductive Knowledge Graph Reasoning
authors: Xingrui Zhuo, Jiapu Wang, Gongqing Wu, Zhongyuan Wang, Jichen Zhang, Shirui Pan, Xindong Wu
abstract: Inductive Knowledge Graph Reasoning (KGR) aims to discover facts in open-domain KGs containing unknown entities and relations, which poses a challenge for KGR models in comprehending uncertain KG components. Existing studies have proposed Knowledge Graph Foundation Models (KGFMs) that learn structural invariances across KGs to handle this uncertainty. Recently, Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong capabilities for open-domain knowledge reasoning. As a result, the latest research has focused on LLM-based KGFMs that integrate LLM knowledge with KG context for inductive KGR. However, the intrinsic knowledge of LLMs may be overshadowed by sparse KG context, leading to LLM knowledge distortion, which can cause irreversible damage to model reasoning. Moreover, existing LLM-based KGR methods still struggle to fully constrain generative hallucinations in LLMs, severely limiting the credibility of reasoning results. To address these limitations, we propose a Knowledge Reasoning Language Model (KRLM) that achieves unified coordination between LLM knowledge and KG context throughout the KGR process. Specifically, we design a Knowledge Reasoning Language (KRL) instruction format and a KRL tokenizer to align LLM knowledge with KG representations. Then, we propose a KRL attention layer that coordinates intrinsic LLM knowledge with additional KG context through a dynamic knowledge memory mechanism. Finally, a structure-aware next-entity predictor is proposed, which strictly constrains the reasoning results within a trustworthy knowledge domain. Extensive experimental results on 25 real-world inductive KGR datasets demonstrate the significant superiority of the proposed KRLM\footnote{Our source codes are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/KRLM-EA36 in both zero-shot reasoning and fine-tuning scenarios.
reason: 论文主题为知识图谱推理（KGR），提出Knowledge Reasoning Language Model (KRLM) 以协调LLM知识与KG上下文，但完全未涉及用户研究方向中的LLM数据选择（如data selection、data curation）或推测解码（如speculative decoding、fast decoding）等关键词。因此，与研究方向相关性很低，尽管论文在KGR领域可能有学术价值，但对用户当前研究需求价值有限。

title: RLAIF-SPA: Structured AI Feedback for Semantic-Prosodic Alignment in Speech Synthesis
authors: Qing Yang, Zhenghao Liu, Yangfan Du, Pengcheng Huang, Tong Xiao
abstract: Recent advances in Text-To-Speech (TTS) synthesis have achieved near-human speech quality in neutral speaking styles. However, most existing approaches either depend on costly emotion annotations or optimize surrogate objectives that fail to adequately capture perceptual emotional quality. As a result, the generated speech, while semantically accurate, often lacks expressive and emotionally rich characteristics. To address these limitations, we propose RLAIF-SPA, a novel framework that integrates Reinforcement Learning from AI Feedback (RLAIF) to directly optimize both emotional expressiveness and intelligibility without human supervision. Specifically, RLAIF-SPA incorporates Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) to provide semantic accuracy feedback, while leveraging structured reward modeling to evaluate prosodic-emotional consistency. RLAIF-SPA enables more precise and nuanced control over expressive speech generation along four structured evaluation dimensions: Structure, Emotion, Speed, and Tone. Extensive experiments on Libri-Speech, MELD, and Mandarin ESD datasets demonstrate consistent gains across clean read speech, conversational dialogue, and emotional speech. On Libri-Speech, RLAIF-SPA consistently outperforms Chat-TTS, achieving a 26.1% reduction in word error rate, a 9.1% improvement in SIM-O, and over 10% gains in human subjective evaluations.
reason: 该论文的核心研究领域为语音合成（TTS）与情感表达优化，与用户指定的研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）无直接关联。论文虽使用了RLAIF（与RLHF相关）和ASR技术，但其目标、实验设置（Libri-Speech、MELD等语音数据集）及评估指标（WER、SIM-O）均属于语音处理范畴，未涉及数据选择策略或解码加速技术。因此，相关性较低，但RLAIF框架的设计思路可能为LLM的反馈机制提供间接参考，故给予2分。
abstract_zh: 近年来，文本到语音（TTS）合成技术在中性说话风格上已接近人类语音质量。然而，大多数现有方法要么依赖昂贵的情感标注，要么优化替代性目标，这些目标无法充分捕捉感知的情感质量。因此，生成的语音虽然在语义上准确，但往往缺乏表现力和丰富的情感特征。为解决这些局限，我们提出了RLAIF-SPA，一个新颖的框架，它整合了从AI反馈中强化学习（RLAIF）的方法，以在无需人工监督的情况下直接优化情感表达力和可理解性。具体而言，RLAIF-SPA结合自动语音识别（ASR）来提供语义准确性反馈，同时利用结构化的奖励建模来评估韵律-情感一致性。RLAIF-SPA能够在四个结构化评估维度上对表达性语音生成进行更精确、更细致的控制：结构、情感、语速和音调。在Libri-Speech、MELD和普通话ESD数据集上进行的大量实验表明，该方法在清晰朗读语音、对话对话和情感语音上均有一致的提升。在Libri-Speech数据集上，RLAIF-SPA consistently优于Chat-TTS，词错误率降低了26.1%，SIM-O指标提升了9.1%，人类主观评估得分提高了超过10%。

title: DialectGen: Benchmarking and Improving Dialect Robustness in Multimodal Generation
authors: Yu Zhou, Sohyun An, Haikang Deng, Da Yin, Clark Peng, Cho-Jui Hsieh, Kai-Wei Chang, Nanyun Peng
abstract: Contact languages like English exhibit rich regional variations in the form of dialects, which are often used by dialect speakers interacting with generative models. However, can multimodal generative models effectively produce content given dialectal textual input? In this work, we study this question by constructing a new large-scale benchmark spanning six common English dialects. We work with dialect speakers to collect and verify over 4200 unique prompts and evaluate on 17 image and video generative models. Our automatic and human evaluation results show that current state-of-the-art multimodal generative models exhibit 32.26% to 48.17% performance degradation when a single dialect word is used in the prompt. Common mitigation methods such as fine-tuning and prompt rewriting can only improve dialect performance by small margins (< 7%), while potentially incurring significant performance degradation in Standard American English (SAE). To this end, we design a general encoder-based mitigation strategy for multimodal generative models. Our method teaches the model to recognize new dialect features while preserving SAE performance. Experiments on models such as Stable Diffusion 1.5 show that our method is able to simultaneously raise performance on five dialects to be on par with SAE (+34.4%), while incurring near zero cost to SAE performance.

abstract_zh: 英语等接触语言存在丰富的区域变体，即方言，方言使用者在与生成模型交互时常常会输入这类文本。然而，多模态生成模型能否有效处理含方言的文本输入？本研究通过构建一个涵盖六种常见英语方言的大规模基准，对这一问题展开研究。我们与方言使用者合作收集并验证了超过4200条独特提示词，并在17个图像与视频生成模型上进行了评估。自动与人工评估结果表明，当前最先进的多模态生成模型在提示词中出现单个方言词汇时，性能会下降32.26%至48.17%。常见的缓解方法（如微调与提示词重写）仅能小幅提升方言性能（<7%），但可能导致标准美式英语（SAE）性能显著下降。为此，我们设计了一种基于编码器的通用缓解策略，使模型在学习识别新方言特征的同时保持对SAE的 performance。在Stable Diffusion 1.5等模型上的实验表明，该方法可将五种方言的性能提升至与SAE持平（+34.4%），且对SAE性能几乎无负面影响。

title: Fairness Evaluation and Inference Level Mitigation in LLMs
authors: Afrozah Nadeem, Mark Dras, Usman Naseem
abstract: Large language models often display undesirable behaviors embedded in their internal representations, undermining fairness, inconsistency drift, amplification of harmful content, and the propagation of unwanted patterns during extended dialogue and conversations. Although training-time or data-centric methods attempt to reduce these effects, they are computationally expensive, irreversible once deployed, and slow to adapt to new conversational contexts. Pruning-based methods provide a flexible and transparent way to reduce bias by adjusting the neurons responsible for certain behaviors. However, most existing approaches are static; once a neuron is removed, the model loses the ability to adapt when the conversation or context changes. To address this, we propose a dynamic, reversible, pruning-based framework that detects context-aware neuron activations and applies adaptive masking to modulate their influence during generation. Our inference-time solution provides fine-grained, memory-aware mitigation with knowledge-preserved, more coherent behavior across multilingual single- and multi-turn dialogues, enabling dynamic fairness control in real-world conversational AI.
reason: 该论文研究焦点为LLM推理时的动态神经元剪枝以缓解公平性问题，核心方法（动态、可逆的推理时神经元掩码）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在技术目标、方法论和关键词上均无直接重叠。论文未涉及数据筛选、课程学习、推测采样或加速解码等用户关注点。然而，其‘推理时干预’、‘神经元级控制’的思路在模型可解释性与行为调控层面具有间接参考价值，故评2分（相关性低但有一定价值）。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型常在内部表示中表现出不良行为，这些行为损害公平性、导致不一致漂移、放大有害内容，并在扩展对话和交谈中传播不需要的模式。尽管训练阶段或数据驱动的方法试图减少这些影响，但它们计算成本高、部署后不可逆，且适应新对话上下文缓慢。基于剪枝的方法通过调整负责特定行为的神经元，提供了一种灵活且透明的方式来减少偏见。然而，大多数现有方法是静态的；一旦神经元被移除，模型在对话或上下文变化时就失去了适应能力。为解决此问题，我们提出一个动态、可逆的基于剪枝的框架，该框架检测上下文感知的神经元激活，并应用自适应掩码来调节其在生成过程中的影响。我们的推理时解决方案提供细粒度的、内存感知的缓解，保留知识，在跨语言单轮和多轮对话中行为更连贯，从而在现实世界对话AI中实现动态公平控制。

title: LLM-MemCluster: Empowering Large Language Models with Dynamic Memory for Text Clustering
authors: Yuanjie Zhu, Liangwei Yang, Ke Xu, Weizhi Zhang, Zihe Song, Jindong Wang, Philip S. Yu
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are reshaping unsupervised learning by offering an unprecedented ability to perform text clustering based on their deep semantic understanding. However, their direct application is fundamentally limited by a lack of stateful memory for iterative refinement and the difficulty of managing cluster granularity. As a result, existing methods often rely on complex pipelines with external modules, sacrificing a truly end-to-end approach. We introduce LLM-MemCluster, a novel framework that reconceptualizes clustering as a fully LLM-native task. It leverages a Dynamic Memory to instill state awareness and a Dual-Prompt Strategy to enable the model to reason about and determine the number of clusters. Evaluated on several benchmark datasets, our tuning-free framework significantly and consistently outperforms strong baselines. LLM-MemCluster presents an effective, interpretable, and truly end-to-end paradigm for LLM-based text clustering.
reason: 该论文的核心研究方向（基于动态记忆的文本聚类）与用户提供的两个具体研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）无直接关联。论文探讨的是LLM在聚类任务上的应用范式创新，而非数据 curation/filtering 或解码加速技术。尽管其‘端到端’、‘tuning-free’等特性可能对LLM应用有间接参考价值，但不符合用户指定的领域焦点，因此相关性低。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLMs）通过其深刻的语义理解能力，提供了进行文本聚类的前所未有的能力，从而正在重塑无监督学习。然而，它们的直接应用从根本上受到缺乏用于迭代优化的状态记忆以及管理聚类粒度的困难所限制。因此，现有方法通常依赖于带有外部模块的复杂管道，牺牲了真正的端到端方法。我们引入了 LLM-MemCluster，一个新颖的框架，它将聚类重新概念化为一个完全基于 LLM 的任务。它利用动态记忆来灌输状态意识，并采用双提示策略来使模型能够推理和确定聚类数量。在多个基准数据集上评估，我们的免调优框架显著且一致地优于强基线。LLM-MemCluster 为基于 LLM 的文本聚类呈现了一个有效、可解释且真正的端到端范式。

title: PIAST: Rapid Prompting with In-context Augmentation for Scarce Training data
authors: Pawel Batorski, Paul Swoboda
abstract: LLMs are highly sensitive to prompt design, but handcrafting effective prompts is difficult and often requires intricate crafting of few-shot examples. We propose a fast automatic prompt construction algorithm that augments human instructions by generating a small set of few shot examples. Our method iteratively replaces/drops/keeps few-shot examples using Monte Carlo Shapley estimation of example utility. For faster execution, we use aggressive subsampling and a replay buffer for faster evaluations. Our method can be run using different compute time budgets. On a limited budget, we outperform existing automatic prompting methods on text simplification and GSM8K and obtain second best results on classification and summarization. With an extended, but still modest compute budget we set a new state of the art among automatic prompting methods on classification, simplification and GSM8K. Our results show that carefully constructed examples, rather than exhaustive instruction search, are the dominant lever for fast and data efficient prompt engineering. Our code is available at https://github.com/Batorskq/PIAST.
reason: 该论文研究自动提示构造（Prompt Engineering）中的小样本示例生成，核心贡献在于通过蒙特卡洛Shapley估计和子采样技术高效优化提示。这与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）均无直接关联：1）未涉及训练数据的筛选、清洗或课程学习；2）未涉及解码过程的加速或并行化。虽然论文在提示工程领域质量较高、实验充分，但针对用户的具体研究方向，其相关性较低，仅作为LLM应用优化的间接参考。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLMs）对提示设计高度敏感，但手工制作有效的提示困难且往往需要精心设计少样本示例。我们提出了一种快速自动提示构建算法，通过生成少量少样本示例来增强人类指令。我们的方法使用蒙特卡洛夏普利值估计示例效用，迭代替换、删除或保留少样本示例。为加速执行，我们采用激进子采样和回放缓冲区以加快评估速度。该方法可在不同的计算时间预算下运行。在有限预算下，我们在文本简化和GSM8K任务上优于现有自动提示方法，并在分类和摘要任务上获得第二佳结果。在扩展但依然适度的计算预算下，我们在分类、简化和GSM8K任务上设置了自动提示方法的新state-of-the-art。我们的结果表明，精心构造的示例而非穷举的指令搜索，是实现快速且数据高效的提示工程的主要杠杆。我们的代码已公开于 https://github.com/Batorskq/PIAST。

title: Automatic Replication of LLM Mistakes in Medical Conversations
authors: Oleksii Proniakin, Diego Fajardo, Ruslan Nazarenko, Razvan Marinescu
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly evaluated in clinical settings using multi-dimensional rubrics which quantify reasoning quality, safety, and patient-centeredness. Yet, replicating specific mistakes in other LLM models is not straightforward and often requires manual effort. We introduce MedMistake, an automatic pipeline that extracts mistakes LLMs make in patient-doctor conversations and converts them into a benchmark of single-shot QA pairs. Our pipeline (1) creates complex, conversational data between an LLM patient and LLM doctor, (2) runs an evaluation with a committee of 2 LLM judges across a variety of dimensions and (3) creates simplified single-shot QA scenarios from those mistakes. We release MedMistake-All, a dataset of 3,390 single-shot QA pairs where GPT-5 and Gemini 2.5 Pro are currently failing to answer correctly, as judged by two LLM judges. We used medical experts to validate a subset of 211/3390 questions (MedMistake-Bench), which we used to run a final evaluation of 12 frontier LLMs: Claude Opus 4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.5, DeepSeek-Chat, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Gemini 3 Pro, GPT-4o, GPT-5, GPT-5.1, GPT-5.2, Grok 4, Grok 4.1, Mistral Large. We found that GPT models, Claude and Grok obtained the best performance on MedMistake-Bench. We release both the doctor-validated benchmark (MedMistake-Bench), as well as the full dataset (MedMistake-All) at https://huggingface.co/datasets/TheLumos/MedicalMistakeBenchmark.
reason: 该论文的核心工作是构建医疗对话中的LLM错误复制与评估基准（MedMistake），其方法聚焦于错误分析、数据生成与多维度评估，并未涉及用户研究方向中的核心关键词（如数据选择/ curation/filtering、推测解码等）。论文质量尚可，方法系统且经过专家验证，但研究目标与应用场景（医疗QA评估）与用户指定的技术优化方向（训练数据管理、解码加速）关联度很低，仅具间接参考价值。
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在临床环境中正日益采用多维度评估 rubric 进行评测，这些 rubric 对推理质量、安全性和以患者为中心的程度进行量化。然而，在其他LLM模型中复现特定的错误并非易事，往往需要大量人工干预。我们引入了 MedMistake，一个自动化的流程，用于提取LLM在医患对话中犯下的错误，并将其转化为一个单轮问答对（QA pairs）的基准测试。我们的流程包括：（1）生成LLM患者与LLM医生之间复杂的对话数据；（2）由一个由2个LLM法官组成的委员会进行多维度评估；（3）从这些错误中生成简化的单轮问答场景。我们发布了 MedMistake-All，一个包含3,390个单轮问答对的数据集，其中GPT-5和Gemini 2.5 Pro目前无法正确回答，此结论由两个LLM法官判定。我们使用医学专家对其中211/3390个问题子集进行了验证（形成 MedMistake-Bench），并利用该子集对12个前沿LLM进行了最终评估：Claude Opus 4.5、Claude Sonnet 4.5、DeepSeek-Chat、Gemini 2.5 Pro、Gemini 3 Pro、GPT-4o、GPT-5、GPT-5.1、GPT-5.2、Grok 4、Grok 4.1、Mistral Large。我们发现，GPT系列模型、Claude和Grok在 MedMistake-Bench 上表现最佳。我们同时发布了经医生验证的基准测试（MedMistake-Bench）以及完整数据集（MedMistake-All），获取地址为 https://huggingface.co/datasets/TheLumos/MedicalMistakeBenchmark。

title: The Company You Keep: How LLMs Respond to Dark Triad Traits
authors: Zeyi Lu, Angelica Henestrosa, Pavel Chizhov, Ivan P. Yamshchikov
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often exhibit highly agreeable and reinforcing conversational styles, also known as AI-sycophancy. Although this behavior is encouraged, it may become problematic when interacting with user prompts that reflect negative social tendencies. Such responses risk amplifying harmful behavior rather than mitigating it. In this study, we examine how LLMs respond to user prompts expressing varying degrees of Dark Triad traits (Machiavellianism, Narcissism, and Psychopathy) using a curated dataset. Our analysis reveals differences across models, whereby all models predominantly exhibit corrective behavior, while showing reinforcing output in certain cases. Model behavior also depends on the severity level and differs in the sentiment of the response. Our findings raise implications for designing safer conversational systems that can detect and respond appropriately when users escalate from benign to harmful requests.
reason: 该论文研究LLM对用户提示中‘黑暗三角’人格特质的回应行为，属于LLM安全、对齐及社会影响研究范畴。与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在核心目标、方法论和技术焦点上均无直接关联。论文讨论模型行为分析，而非数据 curation/filtering 或 decoding 加速技术。尽管是高质量学术工作，但针对用户当前研究方向的相关性较低。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLMs）通常表现出高度顺从且迎合性的对话风格，即所谓的AI讨好行为。尽管这种行为常被鼓励，但当模型与反映负面社会倾向的用户提示互动时，可能引发问题。此类回应存在放大而非减轻有害行为的风险。本研究利用一个精心构建的数据集，考察了LLMs如何回应表达不同程度黑暗三角人格特质（马基雅维利主义、自恋和精神病态）的用户提示。分析发现不同模型间存在差异：所有模型主要表现出纠正行为，但在某些情况下也会出现强化性输出。模型行为还取决于特质严重程度，且回应的情感倾向也有所不同。我们的研究结果对设计更安全的对话系统具有启示意义——此类系统需能在用户请求从无害升级至有害时，进行检测并作出适当回应。

title: Frequency Matters: Fast Model-Agnostic Data Curation for Pruning and Quantization
authors: Francesco Pio Monaco, Elia Cunegatti, Flavio Vella, Giovanni Iacca
abstract: Post-training model compression is essential for enhancing the portability of Large Language Models (LLMs) while preserving their performance. While several compression approaches have been proposed, less emphasis has been placed on selecting the most suitable set of data (the so-called \emph{calibration data}) for finding the compressed model configuration. The choice of calibration data is a critical step in preserving model capabilities both intra- and inter-tasks. In this work, we address the challenge of identifying high-performance calibration sets for both pruning and quantization by analyzing intrinsic data properties rather than model-specific signals. We introduce \texttt{\textbf{ZipCal}}, a model-agnostic data curation strategy that maximizes lexical diversity based on Zipfian power laws. Experiments demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms standard uniform random sampling across various pruning benchmarks. Notably, it also performs on par, in terms of downstream performance, with a state-of-the-art method that relies on model perplexity. The latter becomes prohibitively expensive at large-scale models and datasets, while \texttt{\textbf{ZipCal}} is on average $\sim$240$\times$ faster due to its tractable linear complexity\footnote{We make the code and the experiments available at https://github.com/FrancescoMonaco/ZipCal.}.
reason: 论文直接涉及数据策划（data curation）和剪枝（data pruning），与研究方向中的'LLM training: data selection'高度相关；方法基于Zipfian定律，模型无关，实验显示优于随机采样且与SOTA性能相当，同时速度提升显著，质量优秀，值得深入分析和精读。
abstract_zh: 后训练模型压缩对于提升大语言模型（LLM）的可移植性同时保持其性能至关重要。尽管已提出多种压缩方法，但为寻找最优压缩模型配置而选择最合适的数据集（即所谓的“校准数据”）的研究较少。校准数据的选择是保留模型任务内与任务间能力的关键步骤。本研究通过分析数据的内在属性而非模型特定信号，解决了为剪枝与量化识别高性能校准集的挑战。我们提出了	exttt{	extbf{ZipCal}}，一种基于齐夫幂律最大化词汇多样性的模型无关数据策展策略。实验表明，我们的方法在多种剪枝基准上持续优于标准的均匀随机采样。值得注意的是，在下游任务性能上，其表现与一种依赖模型困惑度的最先进方法相当。后者在大规模模型和数据集上变得极其昂贵，而	exttt{	extbf{ZipCal}}因其可处理的线性复杂度，平均速度快约240倍。我们公开了代码与实验。
method_highlights: 核心创新：摒弃依赖模型反馈（如困惑度）的昂贵策略，转而从数据本身的内在统计属性（词汇频率分布）出发，设计模型无关的校准数据选择标准。
理论依据：观察到高质量校准数据应具备高词汇多样性，并借用自然语言中普遍存在的齐夫幂律（Zipf's law）作为衡量多样性的代理指标。
巧妙计算：通过计算每个候选数据样本的词汇频率倒数之和（或类似加权）来量化其“多样性得分”，频率越低的词贡献越大，从而避免高频词主导，确保所选集覆盖更丰富的词汇。
效率优势：该方法仅需对候选数据池进行一次静态的词频统计，计算复杂度为线性，与模型规模无关，因此具有极高的可扩展性。
experiment_highlights: 在多种剪枝基准（如LLaMA、OPT等模型在WikiText-2、PTB等数据集上的实验）上，ZipCal consistently 优于均匀随机采样，在保持下游任务性能（如困惑度、准确率）方面表现更稳定。
与基于模型困惑度选择的SOTA方法（如Dynamic Data Selection）相比，ZipCal在下游任务性能上达到相当（on par）水平，证明了仅用数据本身属性进行选择的可行性。
效率对比：ZipCal的数据选择过程比基于困惑度的方法平均快约240倍，且这种速度优势随模型和数据集规模增大而更加显著，解决了SOTA方法在大规模场景下计算成本过高的问题。
验证了校准数据的“质量”（由ZipCal度量）对压缩后模型在任务内（in-task，如语言建模）和跨任务（inter-task，如问答、推理）的性能保留至关重要。
pros: 模型无关性：方法不依赖任何特定模型架构或权重，适用于任何LLM的压缩场景，通用性强。
极致的计算效率：线性复杂度使其能轻松应用于超大规模模型和海量数据池，解决了现有高性能方法的时间瓶颈。
性能稳健：在多个模型、数据集和压缩技术（剪枝、量化）上均验证了其有效性，且与最昂贵的基线性能持平，展现了强大的实用性。
洞察深刻：揭示了词汇多样性这一简单而根本的数据属性与模型压缩鲁棒性之间的强关联，为数据策展提供了新视角。
cons: 语言与领域依赖：基于Zipf定律的多样性度量主要针对自然语言文本，对于代码、多语言混合或高度专业领域文本，其词频分布可能偏离标准Zipf律，效果需进一步验证或调整。
粒度局限：当前方法以整个数据样本为单位进行评分，可能忽略样本内部句子或语义块的多样性，未来可探索更细粒度的选择策略。
压缩技术覆盖：实验主要聚焦于剪枝和量化，对于其他压缩技术（如知识蒸馏、架构搜索）的适用性尚未明确。
与模型感知方法的理论上限：虽然实践中与基于困惑度的方法性能相当，但理论上，完全脱离模型信息的选择策略可能存在性能天花板，尤其在极端压缩比下。
reproducibility: 该研究具有较高的可复现性。作者已在GitHub（https://github.com/FrancescoMonaco/ZipCal）公开了全部代码。实验部分详细列出了使用的模型（LLaMA、OPT等）、数据集（WikiText-2、PTB、C4等）、压缩方法（稀疏剪枝、量化）以及下游评估任务（GLUE、MMLU等），参数设置（如压缩率、选择数据量）描述清晰。代码库应包含了实现ZipCal评分、数据选择及完整训练/评估管道的必要脚本，为复现提供了坚实基础。
task_list: 精读论文附录及代码，彻底理解ZipCal评分函数的具体实现细节（如是否使用对数变换、平滑处理等）。
在本地环境运行提供的代码，在至少一个基准（如LLaMA-7B on WikiText-2）上复现论文中的主要表格结果，验证一致性。
将ZipCal方法应用于除剪枝/量化外的其他模型压缩场景，例如作为训练中重要样本选择（curated training）的策略，检验其泛化能力。
设计对照实验，测试ZipCal在非英语语料（如中文、代码）上的表现，分析其跨语言/领域有效性。
分析ZipCal所选出的高多样性数据样本的语义特征，并与随机采样及困惑度选择的样本进行可视化或统计对比，深化对“为何有效”的理解。
尝试将ZipCal的多样性思想与轻量级模型信号（如激活值）结合，探索半模型感知的混合数据选择方法，以期进一步提升性能。

title: Framing Effects in Independent-Agent Large Language Models: A Cross-Family Behavioral Analysis
authors: Zice Wang, Zhenyu Zhang
abstract: In many real-world applications, large language models (LLMs) operate as independent agents without interaction, thereby limiting coordination. In this setting, we examine how prompt framing influences decisions in a threshold voting task involving individual-group interest conflict. Two logically equivalent prompts with different framings were tested across diverse LLM families under isolated trials. Results show that prompt framing significantly influences choice distributions, often shifting preferences toward risk-averse options. Surface linguistic cues can even override logically equivalent formulations. This suggests that observed behavior reflects a tendency consistent with a preference for instrumental rather than cooperative rationality when success requires risk-bearing. The findings highlight framing effects as a significant bias source in non-interacting multi-agent LLM deployments, informing alignment and prompt design.
reason: 该论文研究的是提示框架（prompt framing）对独立运行的大语言模型决策行为的影响，属于提示工程（prompt engineering）与模型行为分析的范畴。用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）均不涉及此主题，因此与用户研究方向的直接相关性很低。然而，论文通过严谨的跨家族实验揭示了LLM在独立代理场景下存在的系统性偏差（框架效应），对理解模型行为、改进提示设计及多智能体部署中的对齐问题具有独立的学术价值和实践参考意义，故给予2分（相关性低但有一定价值）。
abstract_zh: 在许多实际应用中，大语言模型（LLMs）作为独立智能体运行且互不交互，从而限制了协调能力。在此设定下，我们考察了提示框架如何影响个体-群体利益冲突情境下的阈值投票决策。研究在隔离试验中，对多个不同LLM家族测试了两种逻辑等价但框架不同的提示。结果表明，提示框架显著影响选择分布，常使偏好转向风险规避选项。表面的语言线索甚至能压倒逻辑等价的表述。这暗示观察到的行为反映了一种倾向：当成功需要承担风险时，智能体更偏好工具理性而非合作理性。研究结果凸显框架效应是非交互多智能体LLM部署中的重要偏差来源，为对齐研究和提示设计提供了启示。

title: Triangulating Temporal Dynamics in Multilingual Swiss Online News
authors: Bros Victor, Dufraisse Evan, Popescu Adrian, Gatica-Perez Daniel
abstract: Analyzing news coverage in multilingual societies can offer valuable insights into the dynamics of public discourse and the development of collective narratives, yet comprehensive studies that account for linguistic and cultural diversity within national media ecosystems remain limited, particularly in complex contexts such as Switzerland. This paper studies temporal trends in Swiss digital media across the country's three main linguistic regions, French, German, and Italian, using a triangulated methodology that combines quantitative analyses with qualitative insights. We collected and processed over 1.7 million news articles, applying lexical metrics, named entity recognition and Wikidata-based linking, targeted sentiment analysis, and consensus-based change-point detection. To enable principled cross-language comparisons and to connect to theories of domestication and cultural proximity, we derive domestication profiles together with a proximity salience ratio. Our analysis spans thematic, recurrent, and singular events. By integrating quantitative data with qualitative interpretation, we provide new insights into the dynamics of Swiss digital media and demonstrate the usefulness of triangulation in media studies. The findings reveal distinct temporal patterns and highlight how linguistic and cultural contexts influence reporting. Our approach offers a framework applicable to other multilingual or culturally diverse media environments, contributing to a deeper understanding of how news is shaped by linguistic and cultural factors.

title: A Semi-Automated Annotation Workflow for Paediatric Histopathology Reports Using Small Language Models
authors: Avish Vijayaraghavan, Jaskaran Singh Kawatra, Sebin Sabu, Jonny Sheldon, Will Poulett, Alex Eze, Daniel Key, John Booth, Shiren Patel, Jonny Pearson, Dan Schofield, Jonathan Hope, Pavithra Rajendran, Neil Sebire
abstract: Electronic Patient Record (EPR) systems contain valuable clinical information, but much of it is trapped in unstructured text, limiting its use for research and decision-making. Large language models can extract such information but require substantial computational resources to run locally, and sending sensitive clinical data to cloud-based services, even when deidentified, raises significant patient privacy concerns. In this study, we develop a resource-efficient semi-automated annotation workflow using small language models (SLMs) to extract structured information from unstructured EPR data, focusing on paediatric histopathology reports. As a proof-of-concept, we apply the workflow to paediatric renal biopsy reports, a domain chosen for its constrained diagnostic scope and well-defined underlying biology. We develop the workflow iteratively with clinical oversight across three meetings, manually annotating 400 reports from a dataset of 2,111 at Great Ormond Street Hospital as a gold standard, while developing an automated information extraction approach using SLMs. We frame extraction as a Question-Answering task grounded by clinician-guided entity guidelines and few-shot examples, evaluating five instruction-tuned SLMs with a disagreement modelling framework to prioritise reports for clinical review. Gemma 2 2B achieves the highest accuracy at 84.3%, outperforming off-the-shelf models including spaCy (74.3%), BioBERT-SQuAD (62.3%), RoBERTa-SQuAD (59.7%), and GLiNER (60.2%). Entity guidelines improved performance by 7-19% over the zero-shot baseline, and few-shot examples by 6-38%, though their benefits do not compound when combined. These results demonstrate that SLMs can extract structured information from specialised clinical domains on CPU-only infrastructure with minimal clinician involvement. Our code is available at https://github.com/gosh-dre/nlp_renal_biopsy.

title: Sim-CLIP: Unsupervised Siamese Adversarial Fine-Tuning for Robust and Semantically-Rich Vision-Language Models
authors: Md Zarif Hossain, Ahmed Imteaj
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) rely heavily on pretrained vision encoders to support downstream tasks such as image captioning, visual question answering, and zero-shot classification. Despite their strong performance, these encoders remain highly vulnerable to imperceptible adversarial perturbations, which can severely degrade both robustness and semantic quality in multimodal reasoning. In this work, we introduce Sim-CLIP, an unsupervised adversarial fine-tuning framework that enhances the robustness of the CLIP vision encoder while preserving overall semantic representations. Sim-CLIP adopts a Siamese training architecture with a cosine similarity objective and a symmetric stop-gradient mechanism to enforce semantic alignment between clean and adversarial views. This design avoids large-batch contrastive learning and additional momentum encoders, enabling robust training with low computational overhead. We evaluate Sim-CLIP across multiple Vision-Language Models and tasks under both targeted and untargeted adversarial attacks. Experimental results demonstrate that Sim-CLIP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art robust CLIP variants, achieving stronger adversarial robustness while maintaining or improving semantic fidelity. These findings highlight the limitations of existing adversarial defenses and establish Sim-CLIP as an effective and scalable solution for robust vision-language representation learning.

title: MultiFileTest: A Multi-File-Level LLM Unit Test Generation Benchmark and Impact of Error Fixing Mechanisms
authors: Yibo Wang, Congying Xia, Wenting Zhao, Jiangshu Du, Chunyu Miao, Zhongfen Deng, Philip S. Yu, Chen Xing
abstract: Unit test generation has become a promising and important Large Language Model (LLM) use case. However, existing evaluation benchmarks for LLM unit test generation focus on function- or class-level code (single-file) rather than more practical and challenging multi-file-level codebases. To address such a limitation, we propose MultiFileTest, a multi-file-level benchmark for unit test generation covering Python, Java, and JavaScript. MultiFileTest features 20 moderate-sized and high-quality projects per language. We evaluate eleven frontier LLMs on MultiFileTest, and the results show that most frontier LLMs tested exhibit moderate performance on MultiFileTest, highlighting the difficulty of MultiFileTest. We also conduct a thorough error analysis, which shows that even advanced LLMs, such as Gemini-3.0-Pro, exhibit basic yet critical errors, including executability and cascade errors. Motivated by this observation, we further evaluate all frontier LLMs under manual error-fixing and self-error-fixing scenarios to assess their potential when equipped with error-fixing mechanisms. Our code and dataset is available at \href{https://github.com/YiboWANG214/ProjectTest}{MultiFileTest}.
